event_comments:
Thank you to all volunteers for helping make this event a success!
Result statuses: NC means non-competitive; DNF means did not finish; MP means mis-punched.
An asterisk before a time marks the champion time.

Class AWT used within the Team score calculation for successful runners
Category AWT used within the Team score calculation for DNF, MP, Disq runners
US Orienteering Scoring rules
A.35.4 Scoring
For each competitor in each Individual Intercollegiate class with a valid result, their score is computed as 60*(competitorâ€™s time)/ (AWT for the class).
For competitors with an OVT, MSP, DNF or DSQ result, their score shall be the larger of 10+[60*(course time limit)/ (AWT for the male class)] and 10+[60*(course time limit)/ (AWT for the female class)] for their team level (Varsity or JV).
A.35.4.2 Team Scoring: The best three scores from each race for each team are combined for a team score. Lowest overall team score wins.
Each team’s 3 lowest individual member scores per day are combined for the team total. Teams with less than 3 team members in that class will not have a team total.
